summary: Stunning November‑1971 Austin show with a smoking Not Fade Away → Goin’ Down the Road jam, captured in a generally clear soundboard mix despite a few splices.
review: The performance is repeatedly praised as one of 1971’s finest – a first‑set Dark Star and an explosive NFA > GDTRFB sequence that many call “off the charts.”  The stereo soundboard source sounds crisp and full, though listeners note occasional clipping, missing transitions, and a splice in the Dark Star/El Paso segment.

ai_rating.rationale: Overwhelmingly positive comments on both musicianship (70% weight) and a mostly clean soundboard mix (30% weight) across >10 reviews give a clear consensus of an excellent show with minor recording quirks.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Stereo SBD is generally crisp and full; occasional clipping at 0:52, a splice/abrupt transition in the Dark Star > El Paso segment, and missing tape sections noted by a few reviewers.

standout_songs: Not Fade Away > Goin' Down the Road Feeling Bad
show_quality.setlist_flow: First set builds with a tight Dark Star and solid rock numbers; second set erupts into the legendary NFA‑>GDTRFB jam, delivering continuous momentum and high‑energy interplay.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s guitar work is repeatedly highlighted – shredding on Cumberland Blues, lyrical investment on Ramble On Rose, and overall “off‑the‑charts” playing.
band_member_comments.Bob: Weir (Bobby) gets praise for vocal harmonies on Brokedown and energetic interplay on Ramble On Rose.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith’s piano is noted as “in the pocket” early in the tour and praised for percussive/fantastic interplay with Bob on Ramble On Rose.
